task: Config-gate the MCP OAuth2 callback tab to stay open after successful auth (default off) instead of auto-closing, for incident diagnostics.

component_scope.label: S
component_scope.affected: Settings config model (Config), MCP OAuth2 callback page/script renderer (_callback_pages.py)

technical_risk.label: M
technical_risk.risk_factors: Change sits inside the MCP OAuth2 authentication callback flow (auth-adjacent surface); alters tab-close/diagnostics behavior on the success path
technical_risk.mitigation: Config-gated via MCP_AUTH_CALLBACK_KEEP_TAB_OPEN, default False preserves existing auto-close UX; change is an additive conditional branch reusing the existing postMessage/diagnostics beacon logic

key_reasoning.reason: OAuth2 callback bridge is part of the authentication flow; bumped from S to M per the auth red flag, though the behavior change is config-gated and off by default.
Two components touched — the Settings config model and the callback page/script renderer — within one self-contained feature (MCP auth callback diagnostics tab).

red_flags_applied: Technical Risk bumped from S to M: change affects the MCP OAuth2 authentication callback flow (auth red flag)
